content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check whether a number can be express as sum of two prime numbers.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer, TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test contain a single positive integer NNN - the number that needs to be checked.


Output Format

For each test case, print:

`YES` if NNN can be expressed as sum of two primes.
`NO` if not.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if N can be expressed as the sum of two prime numbers, otherwise print NO. Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 7 = 2 + 5 (both prime) → YES. 19 = 2 + 17 → YES. 11 cannot be written as sum of two primes → NO.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
NO
YES
NO
YES
YES
NO
YES
YES
YES
NO

solution.approach: Pre‑compute primality of all numbers up to the maximum N among the test cases using the Sieve of Eratosthenes (O(maxN log log maxN)). For each N: • If N < 4, no two primes can sum to N → answer NO. • If N is even, Goldbach's conjecture has been verified for all even numbers up to 10^6, so answer YES. • If N is odd, the only possible representation is 2 + (N‑2). Check if N‑2 is prime using the pre‑computed table; if yes answer YES, otherwise NO. This yields O(1) per query after the sieve.
solution.timeComplexity: O(maxN log log maxN) for the sieve plus O(T) for answering queries.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(maxN) for the boolean array storing primality.
